svg.title: 36 Panel HUB75 LED Wall Build Schematic
svg.desc: Schematic showing 36 HUB75 RGB LED panels in a 6 by 6 array mounted with magnetic screws to steel strips on a rigid backer board, with vertical steel strips, a symmetric front-removable PETG bezel, rear-mounted 5V power supplies, fused distribution, and Raspberry Pi HUB75 signal chains.
svg.text: 6×6 array, 6.30 ft × 3.15 ft, 768×384 px, magnetic screws on steel strips, symmetric removable PETG bezel on backer margin, rear-mounted power/controller
Front View: Scaled Symmetric Bezel Around 6×6 Panel Array
Panels: 1920 mm × 960 mm. Bezel shown at ~30 mm wide: visible, removable, and mounted to exposed backer margin.

g.text: Backer extends beyond panels; bezel screws into exposed margin.

svg.text: This is the backer/front-face plan before panels are installed. Steel strips run top-to-bottom along panel-column edges.

g.text: Steel strips are vertical. Bezel threaded inserts are outside the panel footprint on the exposed backer margin.

svg.text: Depth Cross-Section: Panel, Magnet, Steel, Backer, PETG Bezel
Cut through the left outside edge. Front is left; wall/backer is right. The bezel is a Z/L profile screwed into the backer margin.

g.text: Front/viewer side ← left; rear/wall side → right.
Bezel screws are accessible from the front. Remove bezel segment first, then pull edge panel off magnets.
Use black PETG/ASA segments with the same profile on all sides.
Use slotted screw holes for 1-2mm adjustment.
Backer margin target: 25-40mm outside panel array.
Do not hide screws behind panels; screws must be front-removable.
1. Remove the needed bezel segment from the front.
2. Pull the edge panel off magnetic screws.
4. Reinstall panel; magnets reseat against steel strips.
5. Reinstall bezel segment with slotted adjustment.

svg.text: Rear View: Power Zones, Data Chains, and Mounting Pattern
Four 60A supplies feed four 3×3 quadrants. Grounds are common; +5V stays separated by zone unless using current-sharing hardware.

g.text: Steel strip plan: 7 vertical strips total: left edge, 5 shared column seams, right edge. Use 2" wide if available, 1.5" minimum.
Snake each chain across two rows
• 36 × P2.5 indoor HUB75/HUB75E RGB panels, 320mm × 160mm each, 128×64 px each
• 144 × M3/M4 LED-module magnetic screws, 15mm standoff height, 4 per panel; confirm M3 vs M4 and thread depth before bulk order
• 1 × 1/2" plywood or MDF backer, approx 2000mm × 1040mm target to allow 25–40mm bezel/backer margin around panel array
• Rear stiffness/wall standoff frame: 1×2 lumber, plywood ribs, or 2020 extrusion; provides cleat mount, airflow, and PSU clearance
• 7 × vertical mild-steel strips, 1/16" thick, 1.5–2" wide preferred; each approx 1020–1040mm long
• Steel total length: 7 × 1040mm = 7280mm / 7.28m / 23.9ft; buy three 8ft strips minimum, four for mistakes/extra landing plates
• PETG or ASA printed bezel, segmented with max printed part length ≤10in; black, removable, magnet-attached or screw-attached to exposed margin
• 4 × Mean Well LRS-350-5 or equivalent 5V 60A supplies minimum; 5 recommended if budget allows for derating/spare capacity
• 36 × HUB75 16-pin IDC ribbon cables, one panel-to-panel/controller cable per module position; order extras in short lengths
• 36 × 5V panel power pigtails/cables, one per panel; avoid relying on high-current daisy chains
• 1 × surge protector or switched/fused AC power strip rated for total AC load; use grounded mains wiring and strain relief
• DC distribution: fused distribution blocks or busbar + fuse block per PSU zone; split into 4 zones of 9 panels
• Suggested fusing: one fuse per panel or small group; size based on wire gauge and branch load, not just panel max draw
• Wire: short 5V/GND branches sized for current and voltage drop; common ground across PSUs, do not tie +5V outputs together directly
• Alibaba listing visible specs: 320×160mm, 128×64 px, P2.5, 32scan, 5V, 0.500kg gross each. Confirm HUB75E pinout, max watts, and M3/M4 insert size with seller.
• Magnetic screw standoff target: 15mm unless one-panel test shows lower profile clears ribs/connectors and cable bend radius.
• Steel strips should extend into bezel margins if using magnetic bezel attachment; otherwise add separate steel landing plates/washers under bezel magnets.
